title: Citadel of Qaitbay (Fort St. Julien)
description: Located in Rashid. Mamluk era (15th Century). A coastal fortress built by Sultan Qaitbay, famously where the Rosetta Stone was discovered.

title: Zaghloul Mosque
description: Located in Rashid. Ottoman era. The oldest mosque in Rashid, a complex of colonnades played a central role in the resistance against the Fraser expedition.

title: El-Abbasi Mosque
description: Located in Rashid. Ottoman era (1809). Characterized by its beautiful ornate minaret and dome, situated directly on the Nile bank.

title: Mosque of Dumqsis
description: Located in Rashid. Ottoman era (1704). A 'hanging mosque' accessed by stairs, built above shops, noted for its fine brickwork.

title: Mosque of Abu Mandour
description: Located in Rashid. Ottoman era. Located on a hill (Tell Abu Mandour) South of the city, famous for its leaning minaret and panoramic Nile view.

title: House of Amasyali
description: Located in Rashid. Ottoman era (1808). renowned for its intricate ceilings and mashrabiya woodwork, typical of the Delta style.

title: House of Arab Killy
description: Located in Rashid. Ottoman era (18th Century). Now the Rashid National Museum, this house showcases the governorate's history and Islamic architecture.

title: House of Hassiba Ghazzal
description: Located in Rashid. Ottoman era (1808). A merchant house featuring fine woodwork, distinct reception halls, and traditional Islamic layout.

title: House of Asfour
description: Located in Rashid. Ottoman era (1754). A large residence with a grand durqa'a (central hall) and courtyard.

title: House of El-Mizouni
description: Located in Rashid. Ottoman era (1740). Historic house linked to the notable El-Mizouni family, featuring preserved architectural details.

title: House of Thabet
description: Located in Rashid. Ottoman era. A smaller but well-preserved example of 18th-century domestic architecture near the souq.

title: House of Ramadan
description: Located in Rashid. Ottoman era. Key example of Rashid's unique black and red brick masonry style.

title: House of Moharam
description: Located in Rashid. Ottoman era. Features a distinctive facade and interior ventilation systems typical of the period.

title: House of El-Gamal
description: Located in Rashid. Ottoman era. Known for its external staircase and decorative brickwork.

title: House of Abu Shaheen
description: Located in Rashid. Ottoman era. A major house complex connected to a historic mill.

title: Mill of Abu Shaheen
description: Located in Rashid. Ottoman era. A rare surviving example of a traditional animal-powered grain mill attached to a residence.

title: Hammam Azouz
description: Located in Rashid. Ottoman era. An ancient public bathhouse (hammam) with domed warm rooms, reflecting social life in old Rashid.

title: Bab El-Rasul
description: Located in Rashid. Ottoman era. The site of one of the old city gates, historically significant for trade and defense.

title: El-Mahalli Mosque
description: Located in Rashid. Ottoman era (1721). Large mosque with a unique ceiling structure supported by 99 columns.

title: El-Jundi Mosque
description: Located in Rashid. Ottoman era. A mosque noted for its stylistic similarities to the larger Zaghloul mosque.

title: Paromeos Monastery (Deir El-Baramous)
description: Located in Wadi El Natrun. Coptic (4th Century). The oldest surviving monastery in the Wadi, founded by St. Macarius.

title: Monastery of Saint Macarius (Deir Abu Maqar)
description: Located in Wadi El Natrun. Coptic (4th Century). Home to the relics of St. Macarius the Great and John the Baptist, residence of many Coptic Popes.

title: Monastery of Saint Bishoy
description: Located in Wadi El Natrun. Coptic (4th Century). Contains the incorrupt body of St. Bishoy and a defensive keep (tower).

title: Monastery of the Syrians (Deir El-Surian)
description: Located in Wadi El Natrun. Coptic (6th Century). Famous for its exquisite frescoes, ancient library, and the Tree of St. Ephrem.

title: Church of St. Michael (Paromeos)
description: Located in Wadi El Natrun. Coptic. An ancient church located within the walls of the Paromeos monastery.

title: Kom El-Hisn (Ancient Imu)
description: Located in Kom Hamada. Old/Middle Kingdom. Archaeological mound of the ancient capital of the 3rd Lower Egyptian Nome.

title: Kom Abu Billo (Terenuthis)
description: Located near Tarrana. Greco-Roman/Coptic. Extensive necropolis and town site with catacombs and a temple dedicated to Hathor.

title: Kom El-Giza
description: Located near Kafr El-Dawar. Greco-Roman. Settlement mound revealing industrial and domestic remains from the classical period.

title: Barnougi
description: Located near Damanhour. Pharaonic. Site containing ancient tombs and settlement remains.

title: Kom El-Ahmar (Sawaris)
description: Located near Nubariya. Greco-Roman. Site of ancient baths and settlement ruins closer to the desert edge.

title: Kom Firin
description: Located in Kom Hamada. Ramesside/Saite Period. Notable for a fortified enclosure and temple remains.

title: Edfina Palace
description: Located in Edfina (near Rashid). Royal Era (20th Century). A royal retreat built by Khedive Abbas II and used by King Farouk, surrounded by gardens.

title: Damanhour Opera House
description: Located in Damanhour. Modern History (1930). An architectural landmark originally built as the municipality and cinema, hosting cultural events.

title: Sidi Damanhour Mosque
description: Located in Damanhour. Islamic. The principal mosque and shrine of the city's patron saint.

title: Tell El-Naqula
description: Located in Beheira. Greco-Roman. An archaeological hill located on the route between the Delta and Alexandria.
